0.original: The other extreme, the sensible way to retire, is the I'm-going-to-pitch-ride-in-and-start-fast method.
0.b1: The other way to retire, which is very sensible, is to start working hard immediately.
0.cn: 另一种退休方式，也就是比较明智的做法，是选择立刻投入工作，保持快节奏。
0.ai_notes:
'pitch right in': to start doing something with energy and enthusiasm.
'extreme': one of two opposite, very different ways of doing something.
0.remarks:
'pitch right in': 充满活力和热情地开始做某事。
'extreme': 两种截然不同或极端的做事方式之一。

1.original: One of my finest friends, Lou Gordon, has chosen this way to retire.
1.b1: My good friend, Lou Gordon, chose this way to retire.
1.cn: 我最好的朋友之一，卢·戈登，就选择了这种退休方式。
1.ai_notes: 'finest friends': a way of saying someone is a very close and dear friend.
1.remarks: 'finest friends': 形容某人是非常亲密和珍贵的朋友。

2.original: Lou's retirement several years ago as a vice president of Atlanta's biggest bank was really commencement day for him.
2.b1: When Lou retired from his job as a bank vice president years ago, it was actually a new beginning for him.
2.cn: 几年前，卢从亚特兰大最大银行副总裁的职位上退休，对他来说，那其实是一个新的开始。
2.ai_notes: 'commencement day': usually refers to a graduation ceremony; here it means a fresh start or a new beginning.
2.remarks: 'commencement day': 通常指毕业典礼；此处指全新的开始。

3.original: He established himself as a business consultant, and his pace is amazing.
3.b1: He started working as a business consultant, and he works very fast.
3.cn: 他成为了一名商业顾问，工作节奏非常快。
3.ai_notes:
'established himself': to become successful or well-known in a particular job or field.
'pace': the speed at which someone does things.
3.remarks:
'established himself': 在特定工作或领域变得成功或出名。
'pace': 做事的速度。

4.original: Now in his 60s, he serves numerous clients and is in national demand as a speaker.
4.b1: Now he is over 60, but he still helps many clients and is often invited to give speeches across the country.
4.cn: 如今他已年过六旬，依然服务于众多客户，并受邀在全国各地发表演讲。
4.ai_notes: 'in national demand': when many people across the country want to hire or invite you.
4.remarks: 'in national demand': 全国各地有很多人想要聘请或邀请你。

5.original: One of his special projects is helping to build Pi Sigma Epsilon, a young but fast-growing fraternity for professional salesmen and sales executives.
5.b1: One of his projects is helping to build a fast-growing group for sales professionals.
5.cn: 他特别关注的项目之一是帮助建立“Pi Sigma Epsilon”，这是一个为专业销售人员和销售主管设立的、年轻且发展迅速的组织。
5.ai_notes: 'fraternity': a social or professional organization, often found in universities or specific industries.
5.remarks: 'fraternity': 一种社交或专业组织，常见于大学或特定行业。

6.original: Every time I see Lou, he seems younger.
6.b1: Every time I see Lou, he looks younger.
6.cn: 每次见到卢，他看起来都更年轻了。
6.ai_notes: 'seems younger': used to describe someone who has a lot of energy and vitality despite their age.
6.remarks: 'seems younger': 用来形容某人尽管年纪大，但依然充满活力。

7.b1: He has the spirit of a 30-year-old.
7.cn: 他的精神状态就像个30岁的年轻人。
7.ai_notes: 'a young 30 in spirit': an expression meaning someone acts and feels much younger than their actual age.
7.remarks: 'a young 30 in spirit': 表达某人的行为和感觉比实际年龄年轻得多。

8.original: Few people I know of any age are reaping more from life than this senior citizen who resolved not to go out to pasture.
8.b1: Few people enjoy life as much as this man who decided not to stop working.
8.cn: 我认识的人中，很少有人能像这位决定不“退居二线”的老人这样享受生活。
8.ai_notes:
'reaping more from life': getting more joy, rewards, or satisfaction out of life.
'go out to pasture': an idiom meaning to retire or stop working because you are considered too old.
8.remarks:
'reaping more from life': 从生活中获得更多的快乐、回报或满足感。
'go out to pasture': 习语，指退休或因为被认为太老而停止工作。

9.original: And the Lou Gordons aren't the boring old grumps feeling sorry for themselves because they're old.
9.b1: People like Lou are not boring or sad just because they are old.
9.cn: 像卢这样的人，并不会因为自己老了就变得无聊、脾气古怪或自怨自艾。
9.ai_notes: 'grumps': people who are often unhappy, complaining, or in a bad mood.
9.remarks: 'grumps': 经常不开心、抱怨或情绪不好的人。

10.original: Goals, intense goals, can keep a person alive when nothing else will.
10.b1: Having strong goals can keep a person alive when nothing else can.
10.cn: 当其他一切都无济于事时，目标，尤其是强烈的人生目标，能让人活下去。
10.ai_notes:
'intense': very strong or serious.
'when nothing else will': when no other method or solution works.
10.remarks:
'intense': 非常强烈或严肃的。
'when nothing else will': 当其他方法或解决方案都不起作用时。

11.original: Mrs. D, the mother of a college friend of mine, contracted cancer when her son was only two.
11.b1: Mrs. D, the mother of my college friend, got cancer when her son was only two years old.
11.cn: D夫人是我大学同学的母亲，她在儿子两岁时患上了癌症。
11.ai_notes: 'contracted': a formal way to say someone caught an illness or disease.
11.remarks: 'contracted': 表达某人患上疾病的正式说法。

12.original: To darken matters, her husband had died only three months before her illness was diagnosed.
12.b1: To make things worse, her husband had died only three months before she got sick.
12.cn: 更糟糕的是，就在她被诊断出患病的前三个月，她的丈夫刚刚去世。
12.ai_notes: 'to darken matters': a phrase used to introduce more bad news or make a situation seem worse.
12.remarks: 'to darken matters': 用来引入更多坏消息或使情况看起来更糟的短语。

13.original: Her physicians offered little hope, but Mrs. D would not give up.
13.b1: The doctors did not think she would live, but she did not give up.
13.cn: 医生们认为她希望渺茫，但D夫人没有放弃。
13.ai_notes: 'offered little hope': when doctors believe a patient is unlikely to recover.
13.remarks: 'offered little hope': 当医生认为病人不太可能康复时。

14.original: She was determined that she would see her two-year-old son through college by operating a small retail store left her by her husband.
14.b1: She wanted to see her son finish college, so she ran the small shop her husband left her.
14.cn: 她决心要供儿子读完大学，于是她经营着丈夫留给她的那家小零售店。
14.ai_notes: 'see her son through college': to support someone financially or emotionally until they finish their education.
14.remarks: 'see her son through college': 在经济上或情感上支持某人直到完成学业。

15.cn: 她经历了大大小小多次手术。

16.original: Each time, the doctors would say, just a few more months.
16.b1: Each time, the doctors said she only had a few more months to live.
16.cn: 每一次，医生都说她只剩下几个月的生命了。
16.ai_notes: 'just a few more months': a common way doctors express that a patient has a very limited time left to live.
16.remarks: 'just a few more months': 医生表达病人生命所剩无几的常用方式。

17.ai_notes: 'cured': when an illness is completely removed or healed.
17.remarks: 'cured': 疾病被完全消除或治愈。

18.original: But those few more months stretched into 20 years.
18.b1: But those few months turned into 20 years.
18.cn: 但那几个月的时间竟延续成了20年。
18.ai_notes: 'stretched into': used to describe a short period of time that unexpectedly becomes much longer.
18.remarks: 'stretched into': 用来描述一段短时间意外地变得很长。

19.original: She saw her son graduated from college.
19.b1: She lived to see her son graduate from college.
19.cn: 她亲眼看着儿子从大学毕业。
19.ai_notes: 'saw her son graduated': a way of saying she lived long enough to witness an important milestone in her son's life.
19.remarks: 'saw her son graduated': 指她活得足够长，见证了儿子生命中的重要里程碑。

20.original: Six weeks later, she was gone.
20.b1: Six weeks later, she passed away.
20.ai_notes: 'she was gone': a gentle, respectful way to say someone has died.
20.remarks: 'she was gone': 一种委婉、尊重的说法，指某人去世了。

21.original: A goal, a burning desire, was powerful enough to stave off sure death for two decades.
21.b1: A strong goal was powerful enough to keep her alive for 20 years.
21.cn: 一个目标，一种强烈的愿望，竟然有足够的力量让她多活了二十年。
21.ai_notes:
'burning desire': a very strong, passionate wish to achieve something.
'stave off': to prevent or delay something bad from happening.
21.remarks:
'burning desire': 实现某事的强烈、热切的愿望。
'stave off': 预防或延缓坏事的发生。

22.b1: Set goals if you want to live longer.
22.ai_notes: 'use goals': a direct piece of advice suggesting that setting objectives is a practical tool for health.
22.remarks: 'use goals': 一条直接的建议，暗示设定目标是保持健康的实用工具。

23.original: No medicine in the world, and your physician will bear this out, is as powerful in bringing about long life as is the desire to do something.
23.b1: Your doctor will agree that no medicine is as powerful as the desire to do something.
23.cn: 你的医生也会认同，没有任何药物能像“想要做点什么”的愿望那样，对长寿有如此强大的推动力。
23.ai_notes:
'bear this out': to confirm or support the truth of a statement.
'bringing about': to cause something to happen.
23.remarks:
'bear this out': 证实或支持某项陈述的真实性。
'bringing about': 导致某事发生。
